title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: device active, can use normal plot, grid ggplot commands. can also customize lines fills drawn, using brushes. Brushes mypaint-brushes package, can install via package manager (e.g. apt brew).  want different plot elements look different, can use set_brush() calls. set brush NULL print axis using (close ) standard R graphics:
code: set_brush("tanda/marker-01") barplot(VADeaths, beside = TRUE, col = NA, cex.names = 0.8) set_brush("experimental/bubble") barplot(VADeaths, axes = FALSE,          beside = TRUE, col = palette.colors(5), border = NA,         cex.names = 0.8) set_brush(NULL) axis(side = 2, at = seq(0, 60, 20))

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: Mypaint brushes work well mypaintr (yet). particular, smudging isn’t fully implemented yet. brushes ’ve found good use, .e. neither crazy similar standard R: classic/charcoal classic/coarse_bulk_1 (_2 _3) classic/dry_brush classic/ink_blot classic/ink_eraser classic/kabura classic/pen classic/pencil classic/slow_ink classic/textured_ink deevad/2B_pencil deevad/4H_pencil deevad/chalk deevad/spray2 Dieterle/HalfToneCMY#1 Dieterle/Pencil-_Left_Handed Dieterle/Posterizer experimental/bubble experimental/track experimental/pixelblocking experimental/sewing experimental/small_blot experimental/spaced-blot experimental/speed_blot experimental/subtle_pencil experimental/track kaerhon_v1/inkster_l ramon/2B_pencil ramon/B-pencil ramon/P._Shade ramon/Pastel_1 ramon/Pen ramon/Sketch_1 ramon/Thin_Pen tanda/acrylic-05-paint tanda/charcoal-01 tanda/charcoal-03 tanda/charcoal-04 tanda/marker-01 tanda/marker-05 tanda/oil-06-paint tanda/pencil-2b tanda/pencil-8b

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: way customize plotting set “hand”. brushes change plotted along given path, hands change path , adding jitter, multiple lines human-like quirks:   Combining brushes hands, can turn R graphics sketch.
code: set_hand(hand(bow = 0.03, wobble = 0)) barplot(VADeaths, beside = TRUE, col = NA, cex.names = 0.8) set_hand(hand(bow = 0, wobble = 0.01, multi_stroke = 2)) barplot(VADeaths, beside = TRUE, col = NA, cex.names = 0.8) set_brush("experimental/bubble") set_hand(hand(seed = 1)) filled.contour(volcano, asp = 1, plot.title = "Maunga Whau",                xlab = "Metres North", ylab = "Metres West")

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: one glitch mypaint_device:may spotted, borders fills don’t always match . , rectangle border fills plotted roughly, random roughness computed separately .  draw_rough_* functions two useful things: always fill roughly drawn shapes correctly. can used graphics device, However, note hands can work graphics device, mypaint brushes can used mypaint_device() graphics device. next chunks use knitr’s standard "png" device.  Control lines fills hand argument:
code: set_hand(hand(seed = 1)) plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n") rect(2, 2, 8, 8, col = "green4", border = "black") knitr::opts_chunk$get("dev") #> [1] "ragg_png"  plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n") draw_rough_polygons(c(2, 4, 6), c(4, 2, 6), col = "red") draw_rough_rect(8, 4, 5, 8, col = "blue3", fill_pattern = hatch())  draw_rough_arrows(1, 9, 8, 9, col = "grey40") plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n")  my_hand <- hand(wobble = 0.01, multi_stroke = 2) draw_rough_polygons(c(2, 4, 6), c(4, 2, 6), col = "red", hand = my_hand) draw_rough_rect(8, 4, 5, 8, col = "blue3", hand = my_hand, fill_pattern = hatch())  draw_rough_arrows(1, 9, 8, 9, col = "grey40", hand = my_hand) plot(c(0.01, 0.11), c(0.01, 0.11), type = "n",       xlab = "bow", ylab = "wobble",       mar = rep(0.1, 4))  for (wobble in 1:5 * 0.02) for (bow in 1:5 * 0.02) {   my_hand <- hand(wobble = wobble, bow = bow)   draw_rough_rect(     bow - 0.008, wobble - 0.008,     bow + 0.008, wobble + 0.008,     hand = my_hand,     col = "red"   ) }

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: Use fill_pattern argument fill polygon using hand-sketched lines. mypaintr knows four ways . , work base graphics devices via draw_rough_* functions:
code: plot(0:10, 0:10, type = "n")  draw_rough_rect(0, 1, 4, 5, col = "blue", fill_pattern = hatch()) draw_rough_rect(0, 6, 4, 10, col = "green4", fill_pattern = crosshatch()) draw_rough_rect(6, 1, 10, 5, col = "red3", fill_pattern = zigzag()) draw_rough_rect(6, 6, 10, 10, col = "grey30", fill_pattern = jumble()) text(c(2, 2, 8, 8), c(0.5, 5.5, 0.5, 5.5),       labels = c("hatch", "crosshatch", "zigzag", "jumble"))

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: can still use draw_rough_* functions mypaint_device active. lets use hands brushes. next chunk also shows use different brushes stroke fill:
code: plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n")  set_brush("experimental/bubble", type = "fill") set_brush(NULL, type = "stroke") my_hand <- hand(wobble = 0.01, multi_stroke = 2)  draw_rough_polygons(c(2, 4, 6), c(4, 2, 6), col = "red", hand = my_hand)  set_brush("deevad/ballpen") draw_rough_rect(8, 4, 5, 8, col = "blue3", hand = my_hand, fill_pattern = hatch())  draw_rough_arrows(1, 9, 8, 9, col = "grey40", hand = my_hand)

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: can use ggplot2 mypaint output device:  fine, can better: probably don’t want special brush render white plot background rectangle. might want “normal” elements mixed sketch elements. mypaintr provides element_mypaint_line(), element_mypaint_rect(), geom_mypaint_bar(), geom_mypaint_col() modify brush hand used individual plot elements. default use “normal” drawing.  ’s picture clean background straight grid lines:  can go way set hand brush inside geom_mypaint_bar():  fancy examples:  save output, can either use mypaint_device() dev.() usual, run ggsave("output.png", dev = mypaint_device). latter advantage can preview plot live, though won’t mypaintr customizations save .
code: library(ggplot2)  # At the console, do this: # mypaint_device("output.png")  set_hand(hand()) set_brush("experimental/bubble")  ggplot(diamonds) +    geom_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut)) +     theme_minimal() ggplot(diamonds) +   mypaint_wrap(     geom_smooth(aes(carat, price), se = TRUE),     brush = "deevad/ballpen",     hand = hand(multi_stroke = 2)   ) +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")' set_hand(hand()) set_brush("experimental/bubble")  ggplot(diamonds) +    geom_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut)) +     theme_minimal() +    theme(      # fill=NULL and hand=NULL by default, i.e. no special effects      plot.background = element_mypaint_rect(fill = "white"),      # the same      panel.grid = element_mypaint_line()    ) ggplot(diamonds) +    geom_mypaint_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut, colour = cut),                     brush = "deevad/ballpen",                    fill_pattern = zigzag(density = 12),                    hand = hand(multi_stroke = 2)) +     theme_minimal() set_brush("experimental/hard_blot", type = "fill") ggplot(diamonds) +    geom_violin(aes(cut, price, fill = cut, colour = cut)) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     panel.grid = element_mypaint_line()   )

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: Knitr replays graphics device. make work dynamically updating device within chunks, must install special hook: chunks use mypaint_device, need set chunk options mypaint=TRUE, fig.keep="none". can set chunks like : Don’t set dev explicitly: hook . One limitation produce one plot per chunk. need , try setting following chunk options: may work, long don’t edit device options within single plot.
code: knitr::knit_hooks$set(mypaint = knitr_mypaint_hook()) knitr::opts_chunk$set(   mypaint = TRUE,   fig.keep = "none" ) dev='mypaint_device', fig.ext='png', fig.keep='high', mypaint=FALSE}

title: Authors and Citation
text: David Hugh-Jones. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hugh-Jones D (2026). mypaintr: Plot R Graphics Like Human. R package version 0.0.1, https://github.com/hughjonesd/mypaintr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mypaintr: Plot R Graphics Like a Human},   author = {David Hugh-Jones},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://github.com/hughjonesd/mypaintr}, }

title: mypaintr
text: mypaintr R package lets plot graphics human-like, sketched way, using brushes libmypaint library algorithms “rough” lines polygons. Installation: base R barplot using custom brush, plus hand-drawn axis:  ggplot using custom element:  Sketchy lines using ordinary base R device:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("hughjonesd/mypaintr") library(mypaintr)  # on the command line, do:  # mypaint_device("output.png")  set_brush("tanda/acrylic-05-paint") barplot(VADeaths, axes = FALSE,          beside = TRUE, col = palette.colors(5), border = NA,         cex.names = 0.8)  set_brush(NULL) set_hand(hand(wobble = 0)) axis(side = 2, at = seq(0, 60, 20))  # dev.off() library(ggplot2)  set_brush("experimental/bubble") ggplot(diamonds) +   geom_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut)) +   theme_minimal() +    theme(     plot.background = element_mypaint_rect(fill = "white"),     panel.grid = element_mypaint_line(colour = "grey")   )  # ggsave("output.png", device = mypaint_device) plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", axes = FALSE)  draw_rough_polygons(5 + 3 * sin(2*pi * 1:5/5), 5 + 3 * cos(2*pi * 1:5/5),                     border = "darkred", col = "red3", lwd = 2,                     hand = hand(multi_stroke = 3),                     fill_pattern = crosshatch())  draw_rough_arrows(8, 8.5, 5.5, 5.5, lwd = 2, hand = hand(bow = 0.05)) text(8, 9, "A pentagon", family = "Chalkboard")

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch
code: crosshatch(angle = 45, density = 7, clip = TRUE, padding = 0)

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch
text: angle One two hatch angles degrees. single angle supplied, second pass defaults angle + 90. density Approximate line density lines per inch. Larger values give denser fills. clip TRUE, hatch endpoints stay shape boundary reduce overshoot. padding Inset polygon edge inches. Positive values leave small gap fill pattern boundary.

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch

what: Theme line element with scoped mypaint rendering — element_mypaint_line
title: Theme line element with scoped mypaint rendering — element_mypaint_line
text: Uses current mypaint device drawing, temporarily overrides stroke settings theme line drawn. useful keeping axes, ticks, panel grid lines solid data layers use rough brush rendering.

title: Theme line element with scoped mypaint rendering — element_mypaint_line
code: element_mypaint_line(   brush = NULL,   brush_settings = NULL,   hand = NULL,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   lineend = NULL,   color = NULL,   linejoin = NULL,   arrow = FALSE,   arrow.fill = NULL,   inherit.blank = FALSE,   size = NULL,   ... )

title: Theme line element with scoped mypaint rendering — element_mypaint_line
text: brush Stroke brush preset, installed brush name, JSON brush string, named settings, NULL solid strokes. brush_settings Named settings overriding brush. hand Optional hand-drawn geometry created hand(). colour, color, linewidth, linetype, lineend, linejoin, arrow, arrow.fill, inherit.blank, size, ... Passed ggplot2::element_line().

title: Theme line element with scoped mypaint rendering — element_mypaint_line
text: ggplot theme element.

title: Theme line element with scoped mypaint rendering — element_mypaint_line
code: if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   ggplot2::theme(axis.line = element_mypaint_line()) } #> <theme> List of 1 #>  $ axis.line: <mypaintr_element_line> #>   ..@ colour       : NULL #>   ..@ linewidth    : NULL #>   ..@ linetype     : NULL #>   ..@ lineend      : NULL #>   ..@ linejoin     : NULL #>   ..@ arrow        : logi FALSE #>   ..@ arrow.fill   : NULL #>   ..@ inherit.blank: logi FALSE #>  @ complete: logi FALSE #>  @ validate: logi TRUE

text: mapping, data, position, just, lineend, linejoin, na.rm, show.legend, inherit.aes ggplot2::geom_col(). stat statistical transformation use. Defaults "count". ... arguments passed ggplot2::layer(). fill_pattern Optional fill pattern created hatch(), crosshatch(), zigzag(), jumble(). omitted, bars use simple hatch fill default. brush, brush_settings Stroke brush spec overrides. fill_brush, fill_settings Fill-hatch brush spec overrides. hand Optional hand-drawn geometry applied outline hatch default. stroke_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry outline. fill_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry hatch strokes. auto_solid_bg Reserved future parity device-level style controls.

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand
text: Hand-drawn geometry settings

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand
code: hand(   seed = NULL,   bow = 0.015,   wobble = 0.006,   multi_stroke = 1L,   width_jitter = 0.08,   endpoint_jitter = 0,   hachure_gap = NULL,   hachure_angle = 45,   hachure_angle_jitter = 12,   hachure_gap_jitter = 0.15,   hachure_method = c("parallel", "cross") )

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand
text: seed Optional random seed used repeatable geometry. bow Typical bowing long strokes proportion segment length. wobble Low-frequency path wobble proportion segment length. multi_stroke Number overdrawn strokes use. width_jitter Relative variation line width overdrawn strokes. endpoint_jitter Relative endpoint jitter proportion segment length. hachure_gap Optional gap hatch lines. NULL, default based polygon size used. hachure_angle Base hatch angle degrees. hachure_angle_jitter Random angle variation hatch passes. hachure_gap_jitter Relative jitter hatch spacing. hachure_method Either "parallel" "cross".

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand
text: object describing rough geometry generated.

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand

title: Hatch fill pattern — hatch
text: Hatch fill pattern

title: Hatch fill pattern — hatch
code: hatch(angle = 45, density = 8, clip = TRUE, padding = 0)

title: Hatch fill pattern — hatch
text: angle Base hatch angle degrees. density Approximate line density lines per inch. Larger values give denser fills. clip TRUE, hatch endpoints stay shape boundary reduce overshoot. padding Inset polygon edge inches. Positive values leave small gap fill pattern boundary.

title: Hatch fill pattern — hatch
text: fill-pattern object draw_rough_*() helpers mypaint geoms.

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
text: Jumble fill pattern

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
code: jumble(   angle = 0,   density = 5,   radius = 0.76/density,   wobble = 0.2,   clip = TRUE,   padding = 0 )

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
text: angle Base angle degrees underlying guide lines. density Approximate line density lines per inch. Larger values give denser fills. radius Loop radius inches. Defaults 0.76 / density, loops sized fraction line spacing. wobble Amount irregularity loop shapes, spacing, size. Larger values give less even, varied loops. clip TRUE, split loop paths shape boundary. padding Inset polygon edge inches. Positive values leave small gap fill pattern boundary.

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
text: fill-pattern object draw_rough_*() helpers mypaint geoms.

what: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: returned hook opens mypaint_device() chunk evaluation injects generated PNG files afterward. avoids knitr's normal plot replay path, preserve device-local style changes set_hand() set_brush().

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
code: knitr_mypaint_hook(...)

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: ... Default arguments passed mypaint_device() hook opens device. Chunk-specific overrides can supplied chunk option mypaint.args named list.

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: function suitable knitr::knit_hooks$set().

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: Register knitr::knit_hooks$set(mypaint = knitr_mypaint_hook(...)) enable chunks mypaint = TRUE. Chunks also set fig.keep = "none" fig.ext = "png". chunk explicitly sets dev=, hook skipped knitr's normal device handling used.

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
code: if (requireNamespace("knitr", quietly = TRUE)) {   hook <- knitr_mypaint_hook(brush = "deevad/2B_pencil")   print(is.function(hook)) } #> [1] TRUE

title: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
text: JSON brush string suitable mypaint_device(brush = ...).

title: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
code: if (length(brushes())) {   x <- load_brush(brushes()[[1]])   stopifnot(is.character(x), length(x) == 1L) }

what: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
text: Open libmypaint-backed graphics device

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
code: mypaint_device(   filename = NULL,   file = NULL,   width = 7,   height = 7,   res = 144,   pointsize = 12,   bg = "white",   brush = "ink",   brush_settings = NULL,   stroke_style = NULL,   fill_style = NULL,   fill_brush = NULL,   fill_settings = NULL,   hand = NULL,   stroke_hand = NULL,   fill_hand = NULL,   auto_solid_bg = TRUE )

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
text: filename Output PNG filename. contains \%d, pages numbered. file Deprecated compatibility alias filename. width, height Device size inches. res Resolution pixels per inch. pointsize Base pointsize. bg Background colour. brush Stroke brush preset, installed mypaint brush name, .myb file path, JSON brush string, named settings, NULL solid strokes. brush_settings Named settings overriding brush. stroke_style Legacy override whether stroke drawing uses brush backend solid Cairo rendering. NULL, inferred whether brush NULL. fill_style Legacy override whether fill drawing uses brush backend solid Cairo rendering. NULL, inferred whether fill_brush NULL. fill_brush Optional fill brush spec. Defaults brush supplied. Use explicit NULL solid fills. fill_settings Named settings overriding fill_brush. hand Optional hand-drawn geometry spec applied stroke fill primitives default. stroke_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry spec strokes. fill_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry spec fills. auto_solid_bg Draw large fills match device background using normal Cairo rendering even fill_style = "brush".

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
text: Opens graphics device returns NULL invisibly.

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
code: out <- tempfile("mypaint-basic-", fileext = "-%d.png") mypaint_device(out, width = 4, height = 3, bg = "ivory") plot(   1:10,   col = "steelblue",   pch = 16,   cex = 1.4,   main = "Ink Lines" ) lines(1:10, col = "firebrick", lwd = 3) rect(2, 3, 5, 7, border = "black", col = rgb(0, 0.6, 0.3, 0.25)) text(6, 8, "hello", col = "black") dev.off() #> agg_record_3702395c561  #>                      2  unlink(Sys.glob(sub("%d", "*", out, fixed = TRUE)))  out <- tempfile("mypaint-brush-", fileext = "-%d.png") mypaint_device(   out,   width = 4,   height = 3,   brush = "chalk",   brush_settings = c(tracking_noise = 0.12),   fill_style = "brush",   fill_brush = "pencil",   fill_settings = c(radius_by_random = 0.08) ) plot.new() plot.window(xlim = c(0, 10), ylim = c(0, 10)) polygon(   c(2, 5, 8, 6, 3),   c(2, 7, 6, 3, 1.5),   border = "black",   col = rgb(0.2, 0.7, 0.5, 0.6) ) lines(c(1, 9), c(1, 9), col = "firebrick", lwd = 4) title("Brush Fill") box() dev.off() #> agg_record_3702395c561  #>                      2  unlink(Sys.glob(sub("%d", "*", out, fixed = TRUE)))  out <- tempfile("mypaint-mixed-", fileext = "-%d.png") mypaint_device(out, width = 4, height = 3, brush = NULL) plot(1:5, 1:5, type = "n", main = "Mixed Styles") set_brush("pencil", type = "stroke") lines(1:5, c(1, 3, 2, 5, 4), lwd = 3) dev.off() #> agg_record_3702395c561  #>                      2  unlink(Sys.glob(sub("%d", "*", out, fixed = TRUE)))

what: Update the active mypaintr device style — mypaint_style
title: Update the active mypaintr device style — mypaint_style
text: Update active mypaintr device style

title: Update the active mypaintr device style — mypaint_style
code: mypaint_style(   brush = NULL,   brush_settings = NULL,   stroke_style = NULL,   fill_style = NULL,   fill_brush = NULL,   fill_settings = NULL,   auto_solid_bg = NULL )

title: Update the active mypaintr device style — mypaint_style
text: brush Stroke brush preset, JSON brush string, named settings. brush_settings Named settings overriding brush. stroke_style Either "brush" "solid". fill_style Either "solid" "brush". fill_brush Fill brush preset, JSON brush string, named settings. fill_settings Named settings overriding fill_brush. auto_solid_bg Whether large fills matching device background drawn normally.

title: Update the active mypaintr device style — mypaint_style
text: NULL, invisibly. active device mypaintr, style becomes default next mypaint_device() opened R session.

what: Wrap a grid grob or ggplot layer with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
title: Wrap a grid grob or ggplot layer with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
text: mypaint_wrap() applies temporary mypaintr brush hand settings wrapped object drawn, restores previous device style. can wrap either grid grob ggplot2 layer. makes useful direct grid::grid.draw() workflows ggplot calls ggplot(...) + mypaint_wrap(geom_line(...), ...).

title: Wrap a grid grob or ggplot layer with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
code: mypaint_wrap(   object,   brush = NULL,   brush_settings = NULL,   fill_brush = NULL,   fill_settings = NULL,   hand = NULL,   stroke_hand = hand,   fill_hand = hand,   auto_solid_bg = NULL )

title: Wrap a grid grob or ggplot layer with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
text: object grid grob ggplot2 layer object. brush Stroke brush preset, installed brush name, JSON brush string, named settings, NULL solid strokes. brush_settings Named settings overriding brush. fill_brush Fill brush preset, installed brush name, JSON brush string, named settings, NULL solid fills. fill_settings Named settings overriding fill_brush. hand Optional hand-drawn geometry applied stroke fill default. stroke_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry strokes. fill_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry fills. auto_solid_bg Optional override background-like fills.

title: Wrap a grid grob or ggplot layer with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
text: object general kind object: wrapped grob grid inputs wrapped layer ggplot2 inputs.

title: Wrap a grid grob or ggplot layer with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
code: line <- grid::linesGrob(c(0.1, 0.9), c(0.2, 0.8)) wrapped <- mypaint_wrap(line, brush = "ink", hand = hand())  if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   ggplot2::ggplot(mtcars, ggplot2::aes(wt, mpg)) +     mypaint_wrap(ggplot2::geom_line(), brush = "ink", hand = hand()) }

text: brush Brush preset, installed brush name, JSON brush string, named settings, NULL switch selected type back solid rendering. settings Named settings overriding brush. type rendering channel update: "", "stroke", "fill". auto_solid_bg Optional override background-like fills.

title: Set the active mypaintr hand-drawn geometry — set_hand
text: hand Hand-drawn geometry created hand(), NULL disable selected type. disables rough path perturbation ; disable active brush, note brushes internal wobbly pathing! Use set_brush() well want fully plain, solid rendering. type rendering channel update: "", "stroke", "fill".

title: Set the active mypaintr hand-drawn geometry — set_hand
text: NULL, invisibly. active device mypaintr, selected hand settings become default next mypaint_device() opened R session.
